channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: PNP Monitors Roblox and Other Online Games for Child Exploitation Concerns
item.description: The Philippine National Police (PNP) is currently keeping a close watch on online games like Roblox due to concerns about child exploitation. Roblox has been flagged by authorities after reports surfa...

item.title: Doja Cat and Her Father Dumisani Dlamini Clash on Social Media Over Family Dispute
item.description: Doja Cat's recent performance in Pretoria has been overshadowed by a very public feud with her father, Dumisani Dlamini, who is a renowned South African actor. The drama began when Doja took to TikTok...

item.title: Brainstem Region Linked to High Blood Pressure: New Insights and Treatment Possibilities
item.description: Researchers from the University of Auckland have identified a surprising brain-based cause of high blood pressure, focusing on a small area in the brainstem known as the lateral parafacial region. Thi...

item.title: Toronto Issues Water Safety Advisory Following Heavy Rainfall
item.description: Residents of Toronto and the Greater Toronto Area (GTA) have been urged to stay clear of waterways as heavy rainfall has raised water levels, creating hazardous conditions. The Toronto and Region Cons...

item.title: Iran formally urges Jordan not to allow its territory or airspace to be used for military strikes
item.description: Iran has formally requested that Jordan refrain from allowing its territory or airspace to be used for any military attacks, amid rising regional tensions involving the United States, Israel, and Iran...

item.title: Federal Judge Strikes Down Pentagon's Press Credential Restrictions as Unconstitutional
item.description: A federal judge in Washington, DC, ruled in favor of the New York Times, overturning the Pentagon's restrictive policies on press credentials that had evicted major outlets like the New York Times, Wa...

item.title: Martyrs' Day: Remembering the Heroes Who Made the Ultimate Sacrifice for India
item.description: Martyrs’ Day, observed on March 23, honours soldiers who have sacrificed their lives for the nation, as well as the revolutionary heroes like Bhagat Singh, Sukhdev, and Rajguru who were executed by th...

item.title: Sheikh Rajabu Calls on Muslims to Sustain Ramadan Morals and Peace in Daily Life
item.description: DODOMA: The Regional Sheikh of Dodoma, Dr Mustapha Rajabu, has advised Muslim believers to preserve the values and good behaviour they adopted during the holy month of Ramadan. Speaking during Eid al-...

item.title: Indian markets slump as rupee weakens to record low amid West Asia conflict and high oil prices
item.description: On Monday, the Indian stock market continued to decline sharply amid global tensions and surging energy prices triggered by the ongoing conflict in West Asia. By 10:30 am, the benchmark BSE Sensex had...

item.title: U.S. government shutdown leads to increased responsibilities for airport immigration officers
item.description: The ongoing U.S. government shutdown has led to significant changes in airport operations, with immigration officers taking on an enhanced role to manage traveller flows and security screenings. As th...

item.title: UN Report Warns of Record Heat in 2025 and Its Long-Term Consequences
item.description: According to the United Nations, the planet trapped unprecedented levels of heat in 2025, raising serious concerns about long-term climate impacts. The World Meteorological Organization (WMO) confirme...

item.title: Tanzania inajumuisha miradi ya maji ili kuboresha kupatia maji safi kote nchi
item.description: Serikali ya Tanzania imeongeza matumizi yake ya fedha na mipango ili kuboresha kipimo cha kipimo cha maji kote nchini. Kwa kazi ya Mpango wa Maendeleo ya Sekta ya Maji (WSDP), serikali imemiliki kiasi...

item.title: Sandiganbayan denies dismissal of bribe case against Chinese tycoon
item.description: MANILA, Philippines — The Sandiganbayan has denied the motion to dismiss filed by Chinese gambling tycoon Jack Lam in connection with a P50 million bribery case. Lam allegedly paid this amount to form...

item.title: Arteta's Decision to Stick with Kepa Costs Arsenal in Carabao Cup Final
item.description: Arsenal manager, Mikel Arteta, made a bold decision to keep Kepa Arrizabalaga as the goalkeeper for the Carabao Cup final against Manchester City, despite Arsenal's long wait for a major trophy since ...

item.title: Australia's Involvement and Response to the Middle East Conflict
item.description: As the Middle East conflict intensifies, Australia's role on the global stage is being tested. While Australia is geographically distant from the frontline, its involvement in the region is significan...

item.title: Halsey and Avan Jogia: From Relationship to Engagement
item.description: American singer and actress Halsey, whose real name is Ashley Nicolette Frangipane, is currently engaged to Canadian actor Avan Jogia. The two began dating in 2023 after being spotted together several...

item.title: UK and Global Leaders Respond to Escalating Iran Conflict and Energy Crisis
item.description: The ongoing conflict between the US-Israel alliance and Iran has reached a critical stage, with Iranian threats to target energy and water infrastructure across the Middle East in response to Donald T...

item.title: Global Climate Report Highlights Urgency for Decarbonisation and Climate Action
item.description: The 2025 State of the Climate report, published by the World Meteorological Organization, paints a worrying picture of our planet’s warming state. Carbon dioxide levels are at their highest in recorde...

item.title: Five bodies retrieved from Ekapa mine following tragic mud rush incident
item.description: The bodies of five mineworkers who were trapped underground at the Ekapa Minerals mine in Kimberley have now been retrieved. The tragic incident occurred on February 17 when a mud rush trapped the wor...

item.title: Gospel Singer Karangu Muraya Attends Bishop Muthee Kiengei's JCM Church Service Amid Past Rumours of Tension
item.description: Gospel musician Karangu Muraya and Bishop Muthee Kiengei, the leader of Jesus Compassion Ministries (JCM), seem to have buried the hatchet after a public appearance together at the church on Sunday, M...
